title: A quick start guide to rKolada
text: Kolada contains five different types metadata entities: kpi: Key Performance Indicators municipality: Municipalities ou: Operating Unit, subunit municipalities kpi_groups: Thematic groupings KPIs municipality_groups: Thematic groupings municipalities obtain data using rKolada usually good idea start exploring metadata. rKolada comes convenience functions five mentioned entities. functions names get_[entity]() can called follows. cache parameter allows temporarily store results disk avoid repeated calls API case need re-run code: already familiarised Kolada API (e.g. reading official docs GitHub) can access full metadata API using get_metadata().
code: kpis <- get_kpi(cache = FALSE) munic <- get_municipality(cache = FALSE)

previous_headings: 3. Describe KPIs > N00370: Ekonomisk hållbarhet - Kommunindex
title: A quick start guide to rKolada
text: Kommunindex för tema Ekonomisk hållbarhet baseras på Genomsnittlig allmän pensionsavgift, kr/invånare 20-64 år. Bruttoregionprodukt 1000 kr/invånare, Finansiella kapitalvinster/förluster, medelvärde befolkningen, kronor, Kapitalvinster/förluster vid försäljning av fastighet och bostadsrätt, medelvärde befolkningen, kronor samt Individer med kapitalvinst/förlust, andel (%). Nyckeltalen normaliseras så att alla kommunernas värden placeras på en skala från 0 till 100 där 0 är sämst och 100 är bäst (för vissa indikatorer används inverterad skala). nästa steg vägs de standardiserade indikatorvärdena samman till index på aspektnivå (temat bygger dagsläget på indikatorer inom tre aspekter). Detta görs med medelvärden, samtliga indikatorer vägs samman med samma vikt inom respektive aspekt. Värdena hamnar även på denna nivå intervallet 0 till 100. Därefter vägs indexet på aspektnivå ihop till temanivå enligt samma princip och även dessa värden hamnar mellan 0 och 100. Viktningen är lika stor för samtliga aspekter inom temat. Källa: Tillväxtverkets beräkningar

title: A quick start guide to rKolada
text: settled KPIs interested next step download actual data Kolada. Use get_values() . download data Kolada API need provide least two following parameters: kpi: One vector several KPI IDs municipality: One vector several municipality IDs municipality group IDs period: years data downloaded. ID tags KPIs municipalities can extracted using convenience functions kpi_extract_ids() municipality_extract_ids(): Setting simplify parameter TRUE, , makes results human readable, removing undocumented columns relabeling data human-friendly labels.
code: kld_data <- get_values(   kpi = kpi_extract_ids(kpi_res),   municipality = municipality_extract_ids(munic_res),   period = 1990:2019,   simplify = TRUE )

title: A quick start guide to rKolada
text: Finally, time inspect data:  Note use helper function values_legend() produce legend containing names keys KPIs included graph.
code: # Visualise results library("ggplot2")  ggplot(kld_data, aes(x = year, y = value)) +   geom_line(aes(color = municipality)) +   facet_grid(kpi ~ .) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title = "Gross Regional Product",     subtitle = "Yearly development in Sweden's three\nmost populous municipalities",     x = "Year",     y = "",     caption = values_legend(kld_data, kpis)   )

title: Introduction to rKolada
text: alternative approach downloading data using known IDs use metadata tables construct arguments get_values(). rKolada provides extract_ids family functions passing metadata table argument get_values. typical workflow download metadata (groups ) KPIs /municipalities, use functions like kpi_search() filter tables rows, call get_values() fetch data. example, let’s say want download KPIs describing Gross Regional Product municipalities socioeconomically similar Arboga, small municipality central Sweden:
code: # Get KPIs describing Gross Regional Product of municipalities kpi_filter <- get_kpi() %>%    kpi_search("bruttoregionprodukt") %>%   kpi_search("K", column = "municipality_type") # Creates a table with two rows  # Get a suitable group of municipalities munic_grp_filter <- get_municipality_groups() %>%    municipality_grp_search("Liknande kommuner socioekonomi, Arboga") # Creates a table with one group of 7 municipalities  # Also include Arboga itself arboga <- get_municipality() %>% municipality_search("Arboga")  # Get data grp_data <- get_values(   kpi = kpi_extract_ids(kpi_filter),   municipality = c(     municipality_grp_extract_ids(munic_grp_filter),     municipality_extract_ids(arboga)   ) ) # Visualize results library("ggplot2") ggplot(grp_data, aes(year, value, color = municipality)) +   geom_line(aes(linetype = municipality)) +   facet_grid(kpi ~ ., scales = "free") +   labs(     title = "Gross Regional Product per capita 2012-2018",     subtitle = "Swedish municipalities similar to Arboga",     caption = values_legend(grp_data, kpi_filter)   ) +   scale_color_viridis_d(option = "B") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: rKolada R package wraps Kolada REST API (https://api.kolada.se/v3/) — database 4,000+ Key Performance Indicators Swedish municipalities regions. provides tidy, pipeable interfaces downloading processing Kolada data.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R session devtools:
code: # Check package (equivalent to CRAN check) R CMD check .  # Build package R CMD build .  # Install locally R CMD INSTALL .  # Run tests Rscript -e "testthat::test_local()"  # Regenerate documentation (roxygen2) Rscript -e "devtools::document()"  # Build pkgdown site Rscript -e "pkgdown::build_site()"  # Regenerate internal vignette data (sysdata.rda) Rscript data-raw/vignette-data.R devtools::check()       # Full package check devtools::test()        # Run tests only devtools::document()    # Rebuild docs from roxygen2 comments devtools::load_all()    # Load package for interactive development devtools::install()     # Install from source

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Two core functions handle Kolada API communication: get_metadata() — fetches entity metadata (KPIs, municipalities, groups, OUs) caching pagination get_values() — fetches actual KPI data values automatic pagination optional simplification (joins human-readable names) Query URLs built compose_metadata_query() compose_data_query(). HTTP via httr2. Pagination handled transparently using v3 next_url field — callers never see .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Thin wrappers around get_metadata() entity type: get_kpi(), get_kpi_groups(), get_municipality(), get_municipality_groups(), get_ou().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: entity type follows consistent pattern operations: - *_search() — filter text/regex - *_describe() — print human-readable descriptions - *_extract_ids() — extract ID vector passing get_values() - *_minimize() — remove low-information columns - *_grp_unnest() — expand grouped entities

title: CLAUDE.md
text: public functions return tibbles designed %>% chaining Naming: entity_operation() (e.g., kpi_search, municipality_extract_ids) API failures return NULL warnings (exceptions) kolada_available() guards examples/vignettes offline safety (required CRAN policy) Uses .data pronoun rlang tidy evaluation filter/mutate contexts; string column names select/rename/unnest contexts Roxygen2 v7.3.1 generates man pages NAMESPACE — never edit hand

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/sysdata.rda contains pre-cached API responses used vignettes avoid network calls. Regenerate via data-raw/vignette-data.R.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests use testthat edition 3 live tests/testthat/. Test files: - test-query-composers.R — URL construction (pure, network) - test-utils.R — internal utilities (pure) - test-search.R — *_search() functions synthetic data (pure) - test-kpi.R — KPI operations (pure) - test-municipality.R — municipality operations (pure) - test-groups.R — group unnest/search/extract (pure) - test-data-helpers.R — values_minimize(), values_legend() (pure) - test-api-mocked.R — API functions mocked httr2 responses (offline-safe) - test-api-live.R — live integration tests (skipped API unavailable) kolada_available() guard function wrap test example calls API.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package previously archived CRAN policy violations (network access tests/examples). API-dependent code must wrapped kolada_available() checks must fail gracefully offline.

text: five different types metadata entities Kolada database: "kpi", "kpi_groups", "municipality", "municipality_groups", "ou". every entity corresponding function get_ENTITY retrieves table metadata entity. get_ENTITY functions thin wrappers around get_metadata.

title: Download metadata for a specific entity from the Kolada API — get_kpi
code: get_kpi(   id = NULL,   max_results = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   cache_location = tempdir,   verbose = FALSE )  get_kpi_groups(   id = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   max_results = NULL,   cache_location = tempdir,   verbose = FALSE )  get_ou(   id = NULL,   municipality = NULL,   max_results = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   cache_location = tempdir,   verbose = FALSE )  get_municipality(   id = NULL,   region_type = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   max_results = NULL,   cache_location = tempdir,   verbose = FALSE )  get_municipality_groups(   id = NULL,   cache = FALSE,   max_results = NULL,   cache_location = tempdir,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Download metadata for a specific entity from the Kolada API — get_kpi
text: id (Optional) One several KPI IDs max_results (Optional) Specify maximum number results returned query. cache Logical. TRUE, downloaded data stored local disk place specified cache_location. data already present local disk, data returned instead downloading data API. cache_location store search cached data. Can path directory name function returns path directory called, like getwd. Defaults tempdir. verbose Whether print call Kolada API message R console. municipality (Optional) string vector strings containing municipality codes. getting OU data, can use parameter narrow search. region_type (Optional) Filter municipalities region type. Common values: "K" (municipality), "L" (region).

title: Download metadata for a specific entity from the Kolada API — get_kpi
text: Returns tibble metadata specified entity. rKolada terminology, table returned e.g. get_kpi referred kpi_df can passed functions starting "kpi" kpi_bind_keywords.

title: Download metadata from the Kolada API — get_metadata
text: entity allowed metadata entity. Check allowed_entities() see updated list. title free-form search term exact title entry current entity. Case insensitive. id ID entry current entity. municipality entity "ou", municipality parameter can added narrow search. region_type (Optional) Filter municipalities region type. used entity "municipality". Common values: "K" (municipality), "L" (region). max_results (Optional) Specify maximum number results returned query. cache Logical. TRUE, downloaded data stored local disk place specified cache_location. data already present local disk, data returned instead downloading data API. cache_location store search cached data. Can path directory name function returns path directory called, like getwd. Defaults tempdir. verbose Whether print call Kolada API message R console.

title: Get data from Kolada — get_values
text: Download table data Kolada. Data selected based three metadata dimensions: KPI (ID), municipality (ID) period (years). must supply arguments least two three dimensions. dimension omitted, available data dimension downloaded.

title: Get data from Kolada — get_values
code: get_values(   kpi = NULL,   municipality = NULL,   period = NULL,   ou = NULL,   unit_type = "municipality",   max_results = NULL,   from_date = NULL,   keep_deleted = FALSE,   simplify = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Get data from Kolada — get_values
text: kpi kpis fetched? Can single name vector names. municipality municipalities data fetched? Can single name vector names. period years data fetched? Can one four-digit integers character strings. ou (Optional) Operating Units data fetched? available certain KPIs. unit_type One "municipality" "ou". Whether fetch data Municipalities Organizational Units. max_results (Optional) Specify maximum number results returned query. from_date (Optional) return data updated date. Format: "YYYY-MM-DD". keep_deleted Logical. FALSE (default), rows isdeleted TRUE removed result. simplify Whether make results human readable. verbose Whether print call Kolada API message R console.

title: Get data from Kolada — get_values
text: tibble containing Kolada values metadata.

title: Get data from Kolada — get_values
code: # Download data for KPIs for Gross Regional Product ("bruttoregionprodukt" in Swedish) # for three municipalities if (kolada_available()) {  # If you already know the ID numbers you are looking for, # you can use these directly as argments. # Otherwise, use the get_\*() functions in combination with \*_search() # functions to find good parameter values. grp_data <- get_values(   kpi = c("N03700", "N03701"),   municipality = c("0180", "1480", "1280") )  # To download OU data instead of Municipality data, set the parameter # "unit_type" to "ou". ou_data <- get_values(  kpi = "N15033",  ou = "V15E144001101",  unit_type = "ou" ) }

code: if (kolada_available()) { # Download Kolada data for the first KPI in the list matching the term "Grundskola" (primary school) # for the years 2010-2019 # (omit the parameter "max_results" to actually download all data) kpi_filter <- get_kpi(max_results = 500) %>%   kpi_search("Grundskola")  # Only keep the top row kpi_filter <- kpi_filter[1, ]  # Only download 100 observations # (omit the parameter "max_results" to actually download all data) kld_data <- get_values(   kpi = kpi_extract_ids(kpi_filter),   period = 2010:2019,   max_results = 100 ) }

title: rKolada 0.3.0
text: KPI field rename: auspices column returned get_kpi() now named auspice (matching v3 API schema). ou_publication_date removed: field longer returned v3 API removed kpi_describe() output. is_divided_by_gender: Now returns logical (TRUE/FALSE) instead integer (0/1). remove_undocumented_columns removed: kpi_minimize() longer accepts remove_undocumented_columns parameter API updated proper documentation data properties.

title: rKolada 0.3.0
text: Kolada API v3: API calls now use https://api.kolada.se/v3/ instead http://api.kolada.se/v2/. v2 API shut March 31, 2026. get_municipality() gains region_type parameter filter municipality type (e.g., "K" municipalities, "L" regions). get_values() gains from_date parameter fetch data updated specific date. get_values() gains keep_deleted parameter (default FALSE). Rows marked deleted v3 API filtered default. Pagination now uses v3 next_url field supports 5000 results per page (2000). kolada_available() now resilient v3 response shape changes.

title: rKolada 0.3.0
text: httr replaced httr2: HTTP backend migrated httr httr2. internal change user-facing API differences. urltools removed: longer dependency. Deprecated dplyr::filter_at() dplyr::select_if() replaced modern dplyr::if_any() / dplyr::all_of() equivalents. New internal entity_search() helper eliminates code duplication across kpi_search(), municipality_search(), ou_search(). New internal append_query_params() helper replaces urltools::param_set(). Deprecated .data$ pronoun usage tidyselect contexts replaced string column names. Comprehensive test suite added: 140+ tests covering query composers, utilities, search functions, KPI/municipality/group operations, mocked API responses, live integration tests. Test infrastructure uses testthat edition 3. get_values() get_metadata() now automatically chunk oversized path parameters batches 25 respect Kolada v3 API limit. Previously, passing 25 KPIs, municipalities, periods return error. Chunking transparent — results combined single tibble.
